info.title: repo
info.description: A CLI tool with commands to help maintain .NET codebases.

commands.name: azdo
commands.description: Azure DevOps operations

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: branch
commands.description: Branch commands

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: favorite
commands.description: Marks a branch as a favorite.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

options.name: --access-token
options.description: The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.

options.name: --account
options.description: The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --project
options.description: The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --repo
options.description: The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: branch-name
arguments.description: The name of the branch to mark as a favorite.

commands.name: pushes
commands.description: List recent pushes to a branch.

options.name: --top
options.description: The maximum number of pushes to return. Defaults to 100.

options.name: --before
options.description: Return only pushes that occurred before this timestamp (e.g. 2024-01-15T12:00:00Z).

options.name: --format
options.description: The output format. Defaults to text.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

options.name: --access-token
options.description: The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.

options.name: --account
options.description: The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --project
options.description: The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --repo
options.description: The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: branch-name
arguments.description: The name of the branch to list pushes for.

options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

commands.description: Pull request commands

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: comment
commands.description: Post a comment on a pull request.

options.name: --state
options.description: The state to set the new comment.

options.name: --type
options.description: The type of comment to post. [default: Text]

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

options.name: --access-token
options.description: The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.

options.name: --account
options.description: The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --project
options.description: The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --repo
options.description: The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.

options.name: --pull-request
options.description: The ID of the pull request.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: comment
arguments.description: The comment to post. Markdown format. If not specified, STDIN will be used.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Creates a new pull request.

options.name: --title
options.description: The title of the pull request.

options.name: --description
options.description: The description of the pull request. If an argument for this option is not specified on the command line, it will be pulled in from STDIN.

options.name: --source
options.description: The name of the branch to merge from. This should not include the refs/heads/ prefix.

options.name: --target
options.description: The name of the branch to merge into. This should not include the refs/heads/ prefix.

options.name: --draft
options.description: Whether the pull request is a draft.

options.name: --labels
options.description: Labels to apply to the pull request.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

options.name: --access-token
options.description: The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.

options.name: --account
options.description: The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.

options.description: The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.

options.description: The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.

options.description: Show help and usage information

options.description: The users to add as required reviewers to the pull request. These should take the form of user@domain.com. All users listed after this switch will be required.

commands.name: update
commands.description: Update a pull request.

options.name: --title
options.description: The title of the pull request.

options.name: --description
options.description: The description of the pull request. If an argument for this option is not specified on the command line, it will be pulled in from STDIN.

options.name: --target-branch
options.description: The target branch of the pull request. This MAY include the refs/heads/ prefix.

options.name: --auto-complete
options.description: Configures the pull request to be automatically completed when all policies are satisfied.

options.name: --merge-strategy
options.description: Specifies the merge strategy to use for auto-complete.

options.name: --delete-source-branch
options.description: Whether to delete the source branch after the pull request is completed.

options.name: --status
options.description: Activates or abandons the pull request.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

commands.description: Create a new work item.

options.name: --area
options.description: The area path for the work item.

options.name: --iteration
options.description: The iteration path for the work item.

options.name: --assigned-to
options.description: The identity to assign the work item to. This will typically be in the form user@domain.com.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

commands.name: git
commands.description: Git repo maintenance workflows

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: trim
commands.description: Removes local branches that have already been merged into some target ref. Squashed branches can sometimes also be detected.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Prints what would be done without actually doing it.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: mergeTarget
arguments.description: Branches become trimmable after they have been merged into this ref. Typically origin/main or similar.

commands.name: nuget
commands.description: NuGet maintenance commands

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: ManagePackageVersionsCentrally
commands.description: Migrates a repo to use centralized package versions.

options.name: --path
options.description: The path to the project or repo to upgrade. [default: <path>]

options.name: --repo-root
options.description: The path to the directory where the Directory.Packages.props is to be created. Defaults to the git repo root above the location specified by --path.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: reconcile-versions
commands.description: Resolves all package downgrade warnings.

options.name: --path
options.description: The path to the project or repo to resolve version issues with.

options.name: --framework
options.description: The target framework used to evaluate package dependencies. [default: netstandard2.0]

options.name: --disregard-version-properties
options.description: Specifies one or more MSBuild properties that may be used to define a PackageVersion item's Version attribute that should no longer be referenced. This may be useful when properties have been used for multiple packages and their continued use is problematic because the packages now need their own distinct versions.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: trim
commands.description: Removes PackageReference items that are redundant because they are to packages that already appear as transitive dependencies.

options.name: --path
options.description: The path to the project file with the PackageReference items to trim.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: upgrade
commands.description: Upgrade a package dependency, and all transitive dependencies such that no package downgrade warnings occur.

options.name: --path
options.description: The path to the project or repo to upgrade.

options.name: --framework
options.description: The target framework used to evaluate package dependencies. [default: netstandard2.0]

options.name: --explode
options.description: Add PackageVersion items for every transitive dependency, so that they can be added as direct project dependencies as versions are pre-specified.

options.name: --disregard-version-properties
options.description: Specifies one or more MSBuild properties that may be used to define a PackageVersion item's Version attribute that should no longer be referenced. This may be useful when properties have been used for multiple packages and their continued use is problematic because the packages now need their own distinct versions.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: The ID of the root package to be upgraded.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information
